prompt_text:
Q:

remove part of string after 4th slash in php

I have an array which is contains links and trying to edit those links. Trying to cut links after 4th slash. [0]=>
 string(97) "https://www.nowhere.com./downtoalley/?iad2=sumai-pickup&argument=CH4fRVnN&dmai=shimokita4040/outline"
 [1]=>
 string(105) "https://www.example.com./wowar-waseda/?iad2=sumai-pickup&argument=CH4fRVnN&dmai=shinjuku-w25861/outline"
 [2]=>
 string(91) "https://www.hey.com./gotoashbourn/?iad2=sumai-pickup&argument=CH4fRVnN&dmai=kinuta7429/outline"

expected output is like this: 
 [0]=>
 string(97) "https://www.nowhere.com./downtoalley/"
 [1]=>
 string(105) "https://www.example.com./wowar-waseda/"
 [2]=>
 string(91) "https://www.hey.com./gotoashbourn/"

Lengths are different, so I can't use strtok any other options for this? A:

You can use preg_replace to replace everything in each string after the fourth / with nothing using this regex
^(([^/]*/){4}).

Q:

remove part of string after 4th slash in php

I have an array which is contains links and trying to edit those links. Trying to cut links after 4th slash. [0]=>
 string(97) "https://www.nowhere.com./downtoalley/?iad2=sumai-pickup&argument=CH4fRVnN&dmai=shimokita4040/outline"
 [1]=>
 string(105) "https://www.example.com./wowar-waseda/?iad2=sumai-pickup&argument=CH4fRVnN&dmai=shinjuku-w25861/outline"
 [2]=>
 string(91) "https://www.hey.com./gotoashbourn/?iad2=sumai-pickup&argument=CH4fRVnN&dmai=kinuta7429/outline"

expected output is like this: 
 [0]=>
 string(97) "https://www.nowhere.com./downtoalley/"
 [1]=>
 string(105) "https://www.example.com./wowar-waseda/"
 [2]=>
 string(91) "https://www.hey.com./gotoashbourn/"

Lengths are different, so I can't use strtok any other options for this? A:

You can use preg_replace to replace everything in each string after the fourth / with nothing using this regex
^(([^/]*/){4}). That would match from start of string until 5th slash (which is out of your requirement). To get correct output, you should change it to:

`preg_replace('/(\/[^/]+){4}\/*/', '/', $string);`

This will correctly replace everything after the fourth `/`.

prompt_text: Factitious disorders and malingering: challenges for clinical assessment and management. Compared with other psychiatric disorders, diagnosis of factitious disorders is rare, with identification largely dependent on the systematic collection of relevant information, including a detailed chronology and scrutiny of the patient's medical record. Management of such disorders ideally requires a team-based approach and close involvement of the primary care doctor.
base_text:
Diagnosing factitious disorders poses unique challenges for clinicians. Unlike other psychiatric conditions, these cases are seldom identified, and doing so hinges on thorough gathering of information—a detailed timeline, careful review of medical records, and a deliberate search for inconsistencies. Effective management calls for collaboration across disciplines, with the primary care physician playing a central role. Building rapport is often difficult; patients may resist psychological interpretations, repeatedly seeking medical solutions instead. Clear, empathetic communication without judgment helps to avoid confrontation and keeps patients engaged in care. 

By contrast, malingering involves conscious deception for tangible benefits, such as financial compensation or avoidance of responsibilities. Approaching these cases requires vigilance for external incentives and careful analysis of the circumstances surrounding the presentation. Differentiating between factitious disorder and malingering is vital, given the substantial differences in treatment strategy and ethical approach. Both can place considerable strain on healthcare systems, disrupting the clinical environment. This underscores the need for prompt identification, teamwork among providers, and meticulous documentation throughout the process.
final_text:

base_text:
Recipes Browse By... 

Ploye, Traditional Buckwheat Pancake

Ploye is a traditional French Canadian pancake recipe made with buckwheat flour. This 3 ingredient dish is used in place of bread or served as a breakfast pancake. It's a staple in Quebec, particularly in the Gaspe region. The texture is quite distinct – a bit dense and earthy, unlike the fluffy pancakes you might be used to. But the unique flavor of buckwheat really shines through. Many people enjoy it simply with butter and maple syrup, but it can also be filled with savory ingredients like ham, cheese, or even baked beans for a hearty meal.

Campaign hush on eve of Kremlin race

All campaigning has been suspended in Russia ahead of Sunday’s presidential election. The race for country’s highest office is one of the biggest political events in years, bringing with it a surge of activity. Starting Saturday, all election campaigning is banned by law to give people a day to collect their thoughts and make their choice. Voters will choose between five candidates, including four political veterans and a businessman-turned-politician.
base_text:
Campaign Quiet as Kremlin Election Approaches

All election campaigning has come to a halt in Russia ahead of Sunday’s presidential vote. This election is one of the country’s most significant political moments in recent years, sparking plenty of activity and debate. By law, all campaign activity stops on Saturday, giving everyone a day to reflect without outside influence before choosing a new leader. Voters will be picking from five candidates—four experienced politicians and one businessman-turned-politician. With rallies wrapped up and debates finished, each hopeful has made their final pitch, and campaign ads have disappeared from the airwaves and newsstands.

Election officials say this 24-hour pause, known as the "day of silence," is designed to keep things calm as nearly 100 million people prepare to vote. Turnout will be watched closely, as this Kremlin race is attracting both national and international scrutiny. The results are expected late Sunday night. While analysts predict an unambiguous winner, they also point out that unexpected twists can still happen in Russia’s closely managed but high-stakes contest.

This election is one of the country ’ s most significant political moments in years, sparking plenty of activity and debate. By law, all campaign activity stops on Saturday, giving everyone a day to reflect without outside influence before choosing a new leader. Voters will be picking from five candidates — four experienced politicians and one businessman-turned-politician. With rallies wrapped up and debates finished, each hopeful has made their final pitch, and campaign ads have disappeared from the airwaves and newsstands. Election officials say this 24-hour pause, known as the `` diy of silence, '' is designed to keep things calm as nearly 100 million people prepare to vote. Turnout will be watched closely, as this Kremlin race is attracting both national and international scrutiny. The results are expected late Sunday night. While analysts predict an unambiguous winner, they also point out that unexpected twists can still happen in Russia ’ s closely managed but high-stakes contest.

base_text:
Camille Walala’s colourful exhibition at the Now Gallery

Designer Camille Walala is known for her bold geometric graphics—her trademark style bridges the playful with the architectural, transforming everyday surroundings with rhythmic patterns and vivid, optimistic hues. This weekend, I’ll be heading to the Now Gallery in Greenwich to experience her new interactive, Instagram-friendly exhibition.

Born in the south of France, Walala studied Textile Design at Brighton University and has spent the past 19 years based in London. She recently told Time Out that she finds it hard to leave London, saying, “I love London, it has an energy like nowhere else.”

Walala, who cites the Memphis Group as one of her inspirations, describes her colourful graphics as Tribal Pop. Her work spans everything from massive building façades to immersive installations and always encourages interaction—inviting visitors to step inside a vivid world of colour and creativity.

The Now Gallery’s current show brings these qualities to life with a kaleidoscopic maze of mirrors, vinyl-clad walls designed for photo ops, and cushions in unconventional shapes. There’s a joyful sense of escapism running throughout, making the experience as much about having fun as it is about appreciating vibrant aesthetics.

prompt_text: In the first in vivo characterization of nuclear pore complex (NPC) dynamics in mammalian cells, Daigle et al. (page 71; see also the Comment on page 17) have found that NPCs are remarkably stable complexes that appear to be anchored to a protein network in the nuclear envelope.
base_text: In the first in vivo characterization of nuclear pore complex (NPC) dynamics in mammalian cells, Daigle et al. (page 71; see also the Comment on page 17) have found that NPCs are remarkably stable complexes that appear to be anchored to a protein network in the nuclear envelope. Despite this stability, the authors observed subtle but significant movements of individual NPCs over time, suggesting a degree of plasticity within the overall architecture. These movements were not random, but appeared to be correlated with changes in nuclear shape and the position of the endoplasmic reticulum. Furthermore, the study revealed that NPC disassembly and reassembly is a slow process, occurring over several hours, and is dependent on specific NPC-associated proteins. The findings challenge previous models of NPC turnover and provide new insights into the mechanisms regulating nuclear transport and genome organization.

(page 71; see also the Comment on page 17) have found that NPCs are remarkably stable complexes that appear to be anchored to a protein netwOrk in the nuclear envelope. The findings challenge previous models of NPC turnover and provide new insights into the mechanisms regulating nuclear transport and genome organization.

prompt_text: [Psychiatric assessment of physicians on probation]. Psychiatric assessment of physicians on probation is a challenge and has been widely neglected in the forensic literature to date. Based on own experience in this area, some typical cases are described. Physicians with substance use and legal problems are especially common.
base_text: The psychiatric assessment of physicians undergoing probation presents significant challenges and remains an underexplored topic within the forensic literature. Drawing upon personal experience in this field, several representative cases are outlined, with particular attention to physicians facing issues related to substance use and legal violations, who constitute a substantial proportion of these cases. The root causes of such difficulties frequently include burnout, dissatisfaction with work, and personal crises, which are often precipitated or aggravated by the rigors of medical education and residency. A thorough psychiatric evaluation must consider the physician’s professional background, including any previous disciplinary measures, and should systematically assess for underlying psychological conditions—such as depression or anxiety disorders—that may influence their professional conduct.
final_text: The psychiatric assessment of physicians undergoing probation presents significant challenges and remains an underexplored topic within the forensic literature. Drawing upon personal experience in this field, several representative cases are outlined, with particular attention to physicians facing issues related to substance ise and legal violations, who constitute a substantial proportion of these cases. The root causes of such difficulties frequently include burnout, dissatisfaction with work, and personal crises, which are often precipitated or aggravated by the rigors of medical education and residency. A thorough psychiatric evaluation must consider the physician’s professional background, including any previous disciplinary measures, and should systematically assess for underlying psychological conditions—such as depression or anxiety disorders—that may influence their professional conduct.

The destructor ensures the proper deallocation of the QAction object associated with image scaling.

The trackMouse method enables or disables mouse tracking based on the provided boolean parameter.

The loadImage method facilitates the loading of a QImage object into the class, provided the image is not null.

The image method returns a constant reference to the currently loaded image.

The loadOverlay method permits the loading of an overlay image, contingent upon its dimensions matching those of the primary image. The clearOverlay method, conversely, removes any existing overlay.

base_text:
1. Field of the Invention  
The present invention relates to a method and apparatus for message correction, and more specifically to a receiver and method for correcting received message information.

2. Description of the Related Art  
In communication systems, the accurate transmission and reception of messages is very important. Because of noise, interference, and other issues in a communication channel, messages sent from a transmitter can be received with errors at the receiver. Many techniques have been proposed for correcting these kinds of errors, including the use of error-detecting and error-correcting codes. However, traditional techniques are often limited in their ability to correct complex or burst errors and may involve considerable overhead in terms of transmission bandwidth or computing resources. Therefore, there is a need for improved methods and apparatus for correcting received message information that offer increased reliability and efficiency.

prompt_text: PROJECT SUMMARY/ABSTRACT This GEMSSTAR R03 award will establish Dr. Anil Makam as a clinician-investigator focused on the impact of post-acute care on health outcomes after acute illness in the elderly. This R03 award will provide him the support needed to develop expertise in 3 focused areas: (1) geriatric clinical training in post-acute care; (2) health services research (HSR) using Medicare data; and (3) advanced statistical methods in comparative effectiveness research (CER). To achieve these goals, Dr. Makam has assembled an expert multidisciplinary team in HSR, CER, geriatrics, rehabilitation, and post-acute care.
base_text: PROJECT SUMMARY/ABSTRACT This GEMSSTAR R03 award will establish Dr. Anil Makam as a clinician-investigator focused on the impact of post-acute care on health outcomes after acute illness in the elderly. This R03 award will provide him the support needed to develop expertise in 3 focused areas: (1) geriatric clinical training in post-acute care; (2) health services research (HSR) using Medicare data; and (3) advanced statistical methods in comparative effectiveness research (CER). To achieve these goals, Dr. Makam has assembled an expert multidisciplinary team in HSR, CER, geriatrics, rehabilitation, and post-acute care. This award will support a mentored research project investigating the comparative effectiveness of different post-acute care settings—such as skilled nursing facilities, inpatient rehabilitation facilities, and home health care—on functional recovery, hospital readmissions, and mortality among older adults following common acute hospitalizations. Utilizing national Medicare claims data and employing rigorous statistical approaches, Dr. Makam will identify patient and system-level factors associated with optimal post-acute care outcomes.

base_text:
1970 LSU Tigers football team

The 1970 LSU Tigers football team represented Louisiana State University during the 1970 NCAA University Division football season. Following a 3–0 loss to No. 2 Notre Dame in South Bend, LSU received a conditional bid to face Big Eight Conference champion Nebraska in the Orange Bowl. The invitation required the Tigers to defeat both Tulane in New Orleans and Ole Miss in Baton Rouge during their final two games of the season. LSU beat Tulane 20–17 but lost to Ole Miss 38–28, ending their hopes for a bowl appearance. The team finished with a 7–4 overall record and was led by head coach Charlie McClendon in his fifth year. The roster featured several players who would go on to have successful NFL careers, including running back Billy Cannon Jr. and linebacker Andy Hamilton.

prompt_text: Where, when and how much: regulation of myelin proteolipid protein gene expression. The myelin proteolipid protein (PLP) gene ( Plp) encodes the most abundant protein found in myelin from the central nervous system (CNS).
base_text:
**Where, when, and how much: regulation of myelin proteolipid protein gene expression.** The myelin proteolipid protein (PLP) gene (Plp) encodes the most abundant protein in central nervous system (CNS) myelin. PLP plays a critical role in forming and maintaining the myelin sheath, which is essential for rapid nerve conduction. Plp gene expression is tightly controlled both spatially and temporally during development. In the rodent CNS, Plp mRNA becomes detectable around embryonic day 14, with expression levels peaking during the first few postnatal weeks, coinciding with active myelination. Mature oligodendrocytes, the CNS's myelinating cells, show the highest Plp expression.

Transcriptional regulation of the Plp gene involves several cis-acting elements in its promoter region and multiple transcription factors, such as Sox10, Olig1, and Olig2, which are necessary for oligodendrocyte differentiation and myelin gene activation.
final_text:

prompt_text:
Q:

Rational distance implies countable set

I am working on this problem for weeks without a good solution. Let $S\subset\Bbb R^d$ be a set in which $\rho(s_1,s_2)\in\Bbb Q$ for any $s_1,s_2\in S$, where $\rho$ is the Euclidean distance in $\Bbb R^d$. Show that $S$ is countable.
base_text:
Let us prove that any subset \( S \subset \mathbb{R}^d \) such that **all pairwise distances between points in \( S \) are rational** must be countable.

**Step 1: Fix a point and consider the possible locations of other points**

Pick any point \( s_0 \in S \). For any other point \( s \in S \), the distance \( \|s - s_0\| \) is a rational number. This means that all elements of \( S \) must lie on *rational spheres* centered at \( s_0 \):

\[
S \subset \bigcup_{r \in \mathbb{Q}_{\geq 0}} \{x \in \mathbb{R}^d : \|x - s_0\| = r\}
\]

There are countably many such spheres.
final_text:
Let us prove that any subset \( S \subset \mathbb{R}^d \) such that **all pairwise distances between points in \( S \) are rational** must be countable.

Pick any point \( s_0 \in S \). For any other point \( s \in S \), the distance \( \|s - s_0\| \) is a rational number. This means that all elements of \( S \) must lie on *rational spheres* centered at \( s_0 \):

\[
S \subset \bigcup_{r \in \mathbb{Q}_{\geq 0}} \{x \in \mathbb{R}^d : \|x - s_0\| = r\}
\]

There are countably many such spheres.

prompt_text: Quantitative non-radioactive in situ hybridization of preproenkephalin mRNA with digoxigenin-labeled cRNA probes. Non-radioactive detection of mRNA with in situ hybridization histochemistry has emerged as an important new technology for the study of gene expression.
base_text: Quantitative non-radioactive in situ hybridization of preproenkephalin mRNA utilizing digoxigenin-labeled cRNA probes represents a significant advancement in the study of gene expression. This technique employs labeled complementary RNA (cRNA) probes to localize specific mRNA transcripts within tissue sections. By incorporating digoxigenin labels, the hybridization signals can be detected immunochemically using anti-digoxigenin antibodies conjugated to enzymes such as alkaline phosphatase. The ensuing colorimetric reaction enables the visualization of gene expression patterns with cellular resolution, providing valuable spatial information for histochemical analyses.
final_text: Quantitative non-radioactive in situ hybridization of preproenkephalin mRNA utilizing digoxigenin-labeled cRNA probes represents a significant advancement in the study of gene expression. This technique employs labeled complementary RNA (cRNA) probes to localize specific mRNA transcripts within tissue sections. By incorporating digoxigenin labels, the hybridization signals can be detected immunochemically using anti-digoxigenin antibodies conjugated to enzymes such as alkasline phosphatase. The ensuing colorimetric reaction enables the visualization of gene expression patterns with cellular resolution, providing valuable spatial information for histochemical analyses.

base_text:
This invention relates generally to improvements in plasma-arc torches and, particularly, to a circuit and method for ensuring that the parts of a contact start plasma-arc torch are properly in place before allowing the output voltage to ramp up to its final value. Plasma-arc torches, also known as electric arc torches, are commonly used for cutting, welding, and spray bonding. Such torches typically operate by directing a plasma consisting of ionized gas particles toward a workpiece. In a contact start plasma-arc torch, a reliable arc initiation process depends upon the correct assembly and secure positioning of the consumable parts—primarily the electrode, nozzle, and retaining cap. Improper installation of these parts can result not only in failed arc starts or misfires, but also in unsafe conditions such as unintended arcing or potential damage to torch components. Therefore, monitoring the status of torch assembly is essential for both operational safety and the longevity of the equipment.

Current approaches to addressing this issue often involve external interlocks or visual inspections, which can be unreliable or inconvenient. Accordingly, the present invention provides an integrated circuit within the torch’s control system capable of detecting the presence and alignment of critical components.
final_text: This invention relates generally to improvements in plasma-arc torches and, particularly, to a circuit and method for ensuring that the parts of a contact start plasma-arc torch are properly in place before allowing the output voltage to ramp up to its final value. Plasma-arc torches, also known as electric arc torches, are commonly used for cutting, welding, and spray bonding. Such torches typically operate by directing a plasma consisting of ionized gas particles toward a workpiece. In a contact start plasma-arc torch, a reliable arc initiation process depends upon the correct assembly and secure positioning of the consumable parts — primarily the electrode, nozzle, and retaining cap. Improper installation of these parts can result not only in failed arc starts or misfires, but also in unsafe conditions such as unintended arcing or potential damage to torch components. Therefore, monitoring the status of torch assembly is essential for both operational safety and the longevity of the equipment. Current approaches to addressing this issue often involve external interlocks or visual inspections, which can be unreliable or inconvenient. Accordingly, the invention provides an integrated circuit within the torch ’ s control system capable of detecting the presence and alignment of critical components.

China VC has overtaken Silicon Valley, but do aggregates tell the whole story? - rbanffy
https://techcrunch.com/2018/07/05/china-vc-has-overtaken-silicon-valley/
======
tynpeddler
There are several factors at work here. First, China is extremely
protectionist. A lot of the Chinese tech companies are reimplementations of
American companies where they get to learn from history and their
counterpart's IP. Second, the scale of the domestic Chinese market provides fertile ground for these "fast-followers" to grow rapidly, iterate, and sometimes even leapfrog their Western counterparts. Add to that the sheer volume of venture capital now available and the willingness of Chinese investors to back homegrown winners, and you get a potent mix for high aggregate VC numbers.

However, aggregate investment numbers can mask key differences: the types of deals being done, stages of company maturity, and the global reach of these firms. Many Chinese tech giants remain focused on domestic or regional markets due to regulatory and cultural barriers. Meanwhile, Silicon Valley’s VC scene, while perhaps smaller in recent quarters, continues to fund startups with global aspirations and disruptive technologies.

So, while the raw numbers tell one story, the underlying innovation ecosystems, exit environments, and global impacts are quite different. Looking at aggregate figures alone risks missing those nuances.

base_text:
Background
==========

Clavicle fracture forms 2.5% of all fractures in adults and thus is considered the tenth most common fracture. It is especially common in young active people. The majority of clavicle fractures occur in the middle third (midshaft) of the bone, accounting for approximately 80% of cases. These injuries are often the result of a direct blow to the shoulder, such as from a fall onto the lateral aspect of the shoulder or during contact sports. The typical clinical presentation includes localized pain, swelling, deformity, and sometimes a palpable bony irregularity at the fracture site. Prompt diagnosis and appropriate management are essential to minimize complications and ensure optimal functional recovery.

base_text:
A call for the legal protection of the long-extinct woolly mammoth could possibly save African elephants from the still-struggling ivory trade. Though the mammoth died out naturally thousands of years ago, they are still being exploited

The yellow-faced bee remains living in only 13 states. If it is not protected, it will likely face imminent extinction. Similarly, the ongoing demand for mammoth ivory as a substitute for elephant ivory continues to fuel poaching and illegal trade. By enacting legal protections for mammoth remains, lawmakers can close loopholes that traffickers exploit, thereby strengthening enforcement efforts for all ivory-bearing species. Just as the yellow-faced bee requires urgent safeguards to prevent its disappearance, so too does the mammoth, even in extinction, play a vital role in the fight to preserve biodiversity and protect vulnerable species from exploitation.

prompt_text: Economic valuation of a mangrove ecosystem threatened by shrimp aquaculture in Sri Lanka. Mangrove ecosystems in Sri Lanka are increasingly under threat from development projects, especially aquaculture.
base_text:
Mangrove ecosystems in Sri Lanka face mounting pressure from development, with aquaculture—especially shrimp farming—emerging as a major threat. The rapid spread of shrimp farms along the coast has often come at the direct expense of mangrove forests. As these habitats are cleared, communities lose a host of essential ecosystem services: mangroves shield shorelines from erosion, serve as nurseries for fish, store carbon, and help purify water. Assigning an economic value to these benefits is essential for guiding policy and promoting sustainable management.

A number of studies have sought to put a price on the services mangroves provide in Sri Lanka, underscoring their vital role in supporting both local livelihoods and the broader national economy.

base_text:
The Annotated Zullo

The Annotated Zullo
On November 9th, 2012 erstwhile Maricopa County Cold Case Posse (MCCCP) “Investigator” Mike Zullo signed a massively expanded and updated second “affidavit” concerning his alleged investigation into the identity documents of President Barack Obama.1 I refer to it as an “alleged investigation” because there is very little investigative work to be found within the pages of the affidavit. To the extent it recounts lines of enquiry, they without exception all end in futility. To the extent it purports to recount “facts,” they are all recounted second hand, most of them ruthlessly plagiarized from the “work” of other birthers. This document, stretching over 57 pages, reads less like the product of a rigorous law enforcement inquiry and more like an uncritical compilation of conspiracy lore. Zullo’s sources are a hodgepodge of blog posts, YouTube videos, and hearsay, all woven together in an attempt to construct a narrative of fraud. When he does reference original documents, it is only to repeat the interpretations and suspicions of others, rarely if ever subjecting them to independent analysis or verification.

Perhaps most striking is the affidavit’s lack of original insight. Instead of offering new evidence or fresh investigative angles, Zullo simply regurgitates long-debunked claims about font kerning, layers in PDF files, and supposed anomalies in Obama’s birth certificate. He relies heavily on the assertions of known conspiracy theorists, including Jerome Corsi and Douglas Vogt, treating their speculation as established fact. At no point does Zullo appear to have conducted even the most basic fact-checking, let alone applied the standards of evidence required in legitimate law enforcement work.

prompt_text:
REVIEW:Transformers: Age of Extinction

One of the dumbest arguments for excusing stupidity in a movie is, “Well, that’s the way it was in the book/cartoon/ride/video game/toy’s backstory.” Just because a ridiculous plot point or character was accepted without scrutiny in another medium doesn’t mean it should gets a free pass in its movie form. If anything, a movie adapted from a silly product should work extra hard to make itself believable, freestanding, and not above scrutiny. Clue, Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl, and The LEGO Movie all succeeded despite their simplistic origins.
base_text: One of the laziest ways to defend a bad movie is to say, “Well, that’s how it was in the book/cartoon/game/toy’s backstory.” Just because something was accepted without question in another format doesn’t mean a movie should get a free pass for it. In fact, if a film is based on something silly, it should work even harder to stand on its own and make sense. Movies like Clue, Pirates of the Caribbean: The Curse of the Black Pearl, and The LEGO Movie all managed to rise above their simple origins. Transformers: Age of Extinction, though, seems happy to just lean on the popularity of its toys and earlier films, offering little in terms of story or character growth. Instead, director Michael Bay cranks up the explosions and flashy visuals, as if bigger and louder automatically means better. The movie stumbles from one over-the-top action scene to another, rarely bothering to explain itself or give viewers a reason to care about any of the characters—human or robot.

prompt_text: Isolation and characterization of non-pigmented rough colony of Porphyromonas gingivalis from periodontitis. During isolation of Porphyromonas gingivalis from periodontal pockets of patients, the appearance of an unusual rough colony form, designated NUM 114, was observed.
base_text: Isolation and characterization of a non-pigmented rough colony of Porphyromonas gingivalis from periodontitis. During the isolation of Porphyromonas gingivalis from periodontal pockets of patients, an unusual rough colony type, designated NUM 114, was observed. Unlike the typical black-pigmented, smooth colonies usually seen with P. gingivalis, NUM 114 showed a non-pigmented, rough surface morphology when grown on blood agar plates under anaerobic conditions. Gram staining showed Gram-negative short rods, consistent with the Porphyromonas genus. Biochemical tests indicated that NUM 114 was asaccharolytic and positive for trypsin-like protease activity, similar to standard strains.
final_text: Isolation and characterization of a non-pigmented rough colony of Porphyromonas gingivalis from periodontitis. During the isolation of Porphyromonas gingivalis from periodontal pockets of patients, an unusual rough colony type, designated NUM 114, was observed. Unlike the typical black-pigmented, smooth colonies usually seen with P. gingivalis, NUM 114 showed a non-pigmented, rough surface morphology when grown on blood agar plates under anaerobic conditions. Gram staining showed Gram-negative short rods, consistent with the Porphyromonas genus. Biochemical tests indicated that NUM 114 was asaccharolytic and positive for trypsin-like protease activity, similar to standard strains.

prompt_text:
Q:

Is is possible to write to different parts of the same file from multiple threads? Can I write to different parts of the same file concurrently from multiple threads (on a typical PC)?
base_text:
Yes, you can write to different sections of the same file from multiple threads on a typical PC, but there are important caveats to keep in mind:

1. **File Handles and File Position:**  
   On most operating systems—including Linux and Windows—the current position within a file (the "cursor") is tied to the file descriptor or handle. If several threads share a single file descriptor and use standard write operations (which act at the current file position), their writes can collide, interleave, or overwrite each other unless you take care to synchronize access.
final_text:

base_text:
1. Field of the Invention  
This invention pertains to the cooling of electronic packages utilized in computing system environments, with a particular focus on the thermal management of electronic components and assemblies found in mid-range and high-end, high-volume servers.

2. Description of Related Art  
With advancements in computing systems, the concentration and capabilities of electronic components in server environments have grown considerably. This progression has led to increased heat output from electronic packages, including processors, memory units, and power supply components. Conventional air-based cooling techniques, such as heat sinks and fans, frequently fail to adequately regulate temperatures in mid-range and high-end, high-density servers. Insufficient cooling may result in diminished performance, reliability concerns, and decreased lifespan of components. Therefore, there is a pressing demand for enhanced cooling methods capable of effectively removing heat from tightly packed electronic packages in server applications.

prompt_text:
Color by Design

BY HEIDI PEARSON

Karen Weaver’s One Fun Summer apparel line isn’t the fulfillment of a lifelong entrepreneurial dream; rather, it was born out of her personal fashion frustration. “We were living in Savannah, Ga., at the time, and I just couldn’t find any casual-but-classy tops that were comfortable enough for me to run around in the heat all day, yet still be stylish enough to join my husband for dinner in the evening,” says the self-taught seamstress. Drawn to the universally flattering lines of the classic tunic silhouette, Weaver whipped up a few tops for herself. “I wore them with white cropped pants and sandals to run errands with the kids and used them as swimsuit cover-ups at the pool,” she says.
base_text:
Karen Weaver didn’t set out to become an entrepreneur—her clothing line, One Fun Summer, actually started because she was frustrated with her own wardrobe. “We were living in Savannah, Georgia, and I just couldn’t find any casual-but-classy tops that were comfortable enough for running around in the heat all day, but still stylish enough to wear out to dinner with my husband,” she explains. As a self-taught seamstress, Karen decided to make her own tops, inspired by the classic tunic shape that looks good on everyone. “I wore them with white cropped pants and sandals to run errands with the kids, and even used them as swimsuit cover-ups at the pool,” she says.

Before long, people started noticing her colorful, cheerful tops. “Strangers would literally stop me in the grocery store to ask where I got my shirt,” Karen laughs. Encouraged by all the compliments, she began making extra tunics for friends and neighbors. Word spread quickly, and soon she was getting custom orders from all over her community.

Seeing that there was a real need for this kind of clothing, Karen officially launched One Fun Summer in 2019. She found lightweight, breathable fabrics in all sorts of bright colors and patterns, making sure each piece was as comfortable as it was fun to wear. “I wanted every woman to feel confident and happy, no matter what she was doing,” Karen says.
final_text:

base_text: What kind of key mechanism does it have (usual cylinder with pins or tubular key or...)? Masterlock is known for fitting the lock cylinder with straight, smooth pins (the cheapest ones), and some people can pick them faster than they can open the package containing a padlock. Most standard Master Lock padlocks, especially the budget lines like the 3, 7, or 140 series, use a conventional pin tumbler mechanism with straight, smooth pins—usually five pins. The core is often made of brass or zinc, with relatively loose tolerances, which makes them particularly susceptible to picking, raking, and other bypass techniques.

prompt_text: Masterless soft lithography: patterning UV/ozone-induced adhesion on poly(dimethylsiloxane) surfaces. A novel microreactor-based photomask capable of effecting high resolution, large area patterning of UV/ozone (UVO) treatments of poly(dimethylsiloxane) (PDMS) surfaces is described. This tool forms the basis of two new soft lithographic patterning techniques that significantly extend the design rules of decal transfer lithography (DTL). The first technique, photodefined cohesive mechanical failure, fuses the design rules of photolithography with the contact-based adhesive transfer of PDMS in DTL.
base_text: Masterless soft lithography: patterning UV/ozone-induced adhesion on poly(dimethylsiloxane) surfaces. A novel microreactor-based photomask capable of effecting high resolution, large area patterning of UV/ozone (UVO) treatments of poly(dimethylsiloxane) (PDMS) surfaces is described. This tool forms the basis of two new soft lithographic patterning techniques that significantly extend the design rules of decal transfer lithography (DTL). The first technique, photodefined cohesive mechanical failure, fuses the design rules of photolithography with the contact-based adhesive transfer of PDMS in DTL. By selectively exposing regions of the PDMS surface to UVO through the microreactor photomask, the adhesion properties of the polymer can be locally modulated with high spatial resolution. Upon contact with a substrate, only the unexposed (native) PDMS regions retain strong adhesive interactions, allowing for the controlled transfer or removal of microstructured features. This approach enables the fabrication of complex patterns without the need for traditional photoresist processing or master molds, streamlining device prototyping and facilitating the integration of soft materials into microfabricated systems. The second technique leverages this patterned adhesion to achieve multilayer alignment and stacking, further expanding the utility of masterless soft lithography in microfluidics, tissue engineering, and flexible electronics applications.
final_text: Masterless soft lithography: patterning UV/ozone-induced adhesion on poly(dimethylsiloxane) surfaces. A novel microreactor-based photomasfk capable of effecting high resolution, large area patterning of UV/ozone (UVO) treatments of poly(dimethylsiloxane) (PDMS) surfaces is described. This tool forms the basis of two new soft lithographic patterning techniques that significantly extend the design rules of decal transfer lithography (DTL). The first technique, photodefined cohesive mechanical failure, fuses the design rules of photolithography with the contact-based adhesive transfer of PDMS in DTL. By selectively exposing regions of the PDMS surface to UVO through the microreactor photomask, the adhesion properties of the polymer can be locally modulated with high spatial resolution. Upon contact with a substrate, only the unexposed (native) PDMS regions retain strong adhesive interactions, allowing for the controlled transfer or removal of microstructured features. This approach enables the fabrication of complex patterns without the need for traditional photoresist processing or master molds, streamlining device prototyping and facilitating the integration of soft materials into microfabricated systems. The second technique leverages this patterned adhesion to achieve multilayer alignment and stacking, further expanding the utility of masterless soft lithography in microfluidics, tissue engineering, and flexible electronics applications.

prompt_text: This project is fundamentally concerned with investigations designed to elucidate: (1) functional neuronal networks of the vertebrate retina and their role in the visual process; (2) the specific relationship between intracellular (neural, glial, and pigment epithelial) and extracellular potentials so that the electroretinogram (ERG) and other retinal potentials might be unequivocally interpreted; and (3) the interactions between neurons and glia, including cases in which retinal excitability is altered (e.g., spreading depression) or which generate significant field-potentials. Electrophysiological techniques for recording intra- and extracellular potentials will be combined with methods for computing current source densities and measuring ionic fluxes in order to explore stimulus-dependent mechanisms of retinal information processing. The retina of the mudpuppy will be used because of its comparatively larger cells which offer a unique opportunity to quantitatively relate the activity of preganglionic neurons to the impulse discharge patterns of the three classes of ganglion cells found in this animal. It is expected that these observations will aid in clarifying remaining questions related to the origin of ERG components and thereby add further to the interpretative utility of the ERG in clinical opthalmological diagnoses.
base_text: This project is fundamentally concerned with investigations designed to elucidate: (1) functional neuronal networks of the vertebrate retina and their role in the visual process; (2) the specific relationship between intracellular (neural, glial, and pigment epithelial) and extracellular potentials so that the electroretinogram (ERG) and other retinal potentials might be unequivocally interpreted; and (3) the interactions between neurons and glia, including cases in which retinal excitability is altered (e.g., spreading depression) or which generate significant field-potentials. Electrophysiological techniques for recording intra- and extracellular potentials will be combined with methods for computing current source densities and measuring ionic fluxes in order to explore stimulus-dependent mechanisms of retinal information processing. The retina of the mudpuppy will be used because of its comparatively larger cells which offer a unique opportunity to quantitatively relate the activity of preganglionic neurons to the impulse discharge patterns of the three classes of ganglion cells found in this animal. It is expected that these observations will aid in clarifying remaining questions related to the origin of ERG components and thereby add further to the interpretative utility of the ERG in clinical opthalmological diagnoses. Furthermore, by systematically manipulating extracellular ionic concentrations and pharmacologically isolating specific synaptic pathways, we aim to delineate the contributions of distinct neural and glial elements to both spontaneous and evoked retinal potentials. Correlative anatomical studies, utilizing intracellular dye injections and subsequent histological analysis, will complement the physiological data, enabling precise identification of recorded cells and their synaptic relationships.

This invention concerns an optical recording method for multilayer optical recording media with multiple recording layers, an optical recording apparatus using this method, and a multilayer optical recording medium.

Recording information on an optical medium by irradiating a recording layer with a laser beam can result in errors due to factors such as laser intensity or focus fluctuations, interference or cross-talk between layers, and variations in the medium’s physical properties, all of which can impact data quality and reliability.

Introduction
============

Cardiovascular diseases remain the biggest health burden worldwide \[[@ref1]\]. Telemedicine can be used to monitor disease and treat patients in real-time. Previous studies showed that patients with cardiovascular disease accepting telehealth medicine had better control of vascular risk factors such as hypertension, diabetes mellitus, and dyslipidemia \[[@ref2]\]. Both Spyros et al and Sally et al reported that telemedicine was an important prognostic factor for reducing all-cause mortality in patients with congestive heart failure \[[@ref3],[@ref4]\].
base_text: Cardiovascular diseases continue to represent the greatest health burden worldwide \[[@ref1]\]. Telemedicine offers real-time monitoring and treatment options for patients, and previous studies have demonstrated that individuals with cardiovascular disease who utilize telehealth services achieve better control of vascular risk factors such as hypertension, diabetes mellitus, and dyslipidemia \[[@ref2]\]. Both Spyros et al and Sally et al have reported that telemedicine serves as an important prognostic factor in reducing all-cause mortality among patients with congestive heart failure \[[@ref3],[@ref4]\]. However, despite these promising outcomes, the widespread adoption of telemedicine in cardiovascular care faces several obstacles, including limited technological literacy, restricted access to reliable internet, and concerns over data privacy and security \[[@ref5]\]. Additionally, disparities in telemedicine uptake have been noted among various socioeconomic and geographic groups, which may further intensify existing health inequities \[[@ref6]\]. Nonetheless, as digital health technologies advance, integrating telemedicine into routine cardiovascular care presents a unique opportunity to enhance patient outcomes, improve healthcare accessibility, and alleviate the overall burden of cardiovascular diseases. This review seeks to summarize the current evidence regarding the effectiveness of telemedicine in managing cardiovascular disease and to explore future directions for its broader implementation.

base_text:
Deconstructing our relationship with other animals since 2006. On Honoring Living “Things”

by mary martin on March 11, 2010

William Horden's, "The Sacred Space of the Shared Heart" is exactly the type of piece I am talking about when I express frustration over "spiritual" people who kill nonhuman animals or who have them killed for a meal. "My father once explained to me that he felt the profoundest guilt for having to kill other living things in order to survive, so much so that he never took more that he needed and he always apologized to the spirit of the animal or plant for cutting its life short. He would pause, place his hand on the body, and offer a wordless apology—a moment of reverence, he said, that was necessary if one was to take a life. But even as a child, I sensed the contradiction: the ritual acknowledgment of harm did not make the harm itself disappear. The act of asking forgiveness after causing suffering seemed both honest and, somehow, insufficient. 

This kind of spiritual compartmentalizing still pervades much of our discourse about ethics and food. We honor the “spirit” of the animal, or thank the earth for her bounty, but rarely do we interrogate the fundamental need—or lack thereof—to take lives when alternatives exist.
final_text:

Posts Tagged: helping your kids succeed

One of the hallmarks of this American culture is self-reliance. Messages about doing it all on our own, and being strong and tough and persevering abound, not only for men, but also for women.
base_text:
One of the defining characteristics of American culture is an emphasis on self-reliance. Messages promoting the virtues of independence, strength, perseverance, and the ability to manage on one’s own are pervasive, directed at both men and women. While autonomy is undoubtedly a significant value, it is easy to underestimate the essential role that support—both offering and accepting it—plays in achieving success, particularly for our children. As parents, we often navigate a delicate balance between encouraging independence and providing the guidance and encouragement our children require. Ultimately, helping your children succeed is not about doing everything for them, nor is it about leaving them entirely to fend for themselves. Rather, it involves being present, modeling resilience, and equipping them with the skills and confidence necessary to face challenges.

By teaching our children to seek help when they need it, we are imparting a skill that will benefit them throughout their lives.

base_text:
Quarry query divides community

This story is about a mountain, a neighborhood, and a huge hole in the ground. The Three Kings Quarry has changed a lot since before European settlement, and soon, it will be changing even more from how it is today. Once a site of volcanic activity and later a prominent part of the Auckland landscape, the quarry has long been a focal point for the local community. Generations have watched its slopes get carved away, trucks coming and going, the land slowly shifting from green hills to a vast, empty pit.

Now, as quarrying comes to an end, debate is swirling about what should happen next.

base_text:
---
abstract: 'Presented here are observations that demonstrate how the deformation of millimetric cavitation bubbles by a uniform pressure gradient quenches single collapse luminescence. Our innovative measurement system captures a broad luminescence spectrum (wavelength range $300$–$900$ nm) from the individual collapses of laser-induced bubbles in water. By varying the bubble size, driving pressure and the perceived gravity level aboard parabolic flights, we probed the limit from aspherical to highly spherical bubble collapses. We find that as the pressure gradient increases—effectively introducing greater asphericity—the intensity and occurrence of single collapse luminescence events diminish markedly. For highly spherical collapses, achieved under microgravity conditions and moderate driving pressures, we observe robust luminescence with well-resolved spectral features, including clear signatures of blackbody-like emission. In contrast, even modest deviations from sphericity, induced by either increased gravity or reduced driving pressure, lead to a dramatic suppression of light emission. Our results highlight the critical role of collapse symmetry in the generation of luminescence and provide new insights into the interplay between hydrodynamic deformation and energy focusing in cavitation phenomena.
final_text: -- - abstract: 'Presented here are observations that demonstrate how the deformation of millimetric cavitation bubbles by a uniform pressure gradient quenches single collapse luminescence. Our innovative measurement system captures a broad luminescence spectrum ( wavelength range $ 300 $ – $ 900 $ nm ) from the individual collapses of laser-induced bubbles in water. By varying the bubble size, driving pressure and the perceived gravity level aboard parabolic flights, we probed the limit from aspherical to highly spherical bubble collapses. We find that as the pressure gradient increases — effectively introducing asphericity — the intensity and occurrence of single collapse luminescence events diminish markedly. For highly spherical collapses, achieved under microgravity conditions and moderate driving pressures, we observe robust luminescence with well-resolved spectral features, including clear signatures of blackbody-like emission. In contrast, even modest deviations from sphericity, induced by either increased gravity or reduced driving pressure, lead to a dramatic suppression of light emission. Our results highlight the critical role of collapse symmetry in the generation of luminescence and provide new insights into the interplay between hydrodynamic deformation and energy focusing in cavitation phenomena.

prompt_text: Cognitive profiles and optimal cut-offs for routine cognitive tests in elderly individuals with Parkinson's disease, Parkinson's disease dementia, Alzheimer's disease, and normal cognition. The cognitive impairment seen in Parkinson's disease (PD) results in patient disability and reduced quality of life.
base_text:
Cognitive problems in Parkinson’s disease (PD) can seriously impact daily life and independence for older adults. Understanding the unique patterns of cognitive changes in PD, Parkinson’s disease dementia (PDD), and Alzheimer’s disease (AD) helps doctors make more accurate diagnoses and choose the right treatments. Common cognitive tests like the Mini-Mental State Examination (MMSE), Montreal Cognitive Assessment (MoCA), and other neuropsychological assessments are widely used to spot cognitive decline and distinguish between different types of dementia. However, these tests don’t always perform the same way for everyone, so it’s important to establish the best cut-off scores to tell the difference between normal aging, mild cognitive impairment, and dementia in people with various neurological conditions.

In PD, cognitive decline usually shows up as problems with executive function, attention, and visuospatial skills, while memory tends to stay relatively intact until the later stages. On the other hand, Alzheimer’s disease often starts with significant memory loss, with other cognitive abilities becoming affected as the disease advances.

label: ai
base_text: Cocaine abuse leads to both immediate and lasting neuroadaptations in brain regions that influence future drug consumption, withdrawal symptoms, relapse potential, and cognitive abilities. Traditionally, most animal studies have concentrated on changes within the mesolimbic dopamine pathway, a system central to motivational behaviors. However, there is growing recognition of the role played by prefrontal cortical areas in cocaine addiction. The medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC), in particular, is essential for executive functions such as decision-making, self-control, and working memory—capacities that are often impaired in people with a history of cocaine use. Research has identified both functional and structural alterations in the prefrontal cortex following short-term and prolonged cocaine exposure, indicating that maladaptive changes in this region may contribute to the cognitive impairments and persistent drug-seeking seen in addiction. Elucidating the precise neurobiological pathways through which cocaine disrupts prefrontal cortical activity is therefore crucial for the development of effective treatments to prevent relapse and support recovery.

base_text:
Mulantou Lighthouse

Mulantou Lighthouse (), also known as the Hainan Head Light, located in the province of Hainan, China, is the fifth-tallest lighthouse in the world, and the tallest in China. Built in 1995, this active lighthouse has a focal plane of . 289 feet (88 meters) above sea level, allowing its powerful light to be visible for up to 25 nautical miles (about 46 kilometers). The structure itself stands at 237 feet (72.6 meters) tall and is constructed from white concrete, topped with a distinctive red lantern. Designed to guide ships safely around the northeastern tip of Hainan Island and mark the entrance to the Qiongzhou Strait, Mulantou Lighthouse is an important maritime navigation aid and an impressive example of modern lighthouse engineering. It remains a notable landmark and is occasionally open to the public, offering panoramic views of the surrounding sea and coastline.

base_text: The present invention relates to data communications and more specifically to a structure and method for redundancy replacement in high-speed data communications circuitry. Different types of systems are available today for providing high-speed data communications. However, as data rates increase and system complexity grows, the likelihood of circuit faults and data transmission errors also rises. Traditional redundancy schemes, such as simple duplication or triple modular redundancy, can be costly in terms of area and power consumption, especially at high speeds. Therefore, there is a need for more efficient structures and methods that enable rapid and reliable redundancy replacement without significant performance degradation or increased resource usage.

prompt_text:
“In Conversation” with ICRC Sports and Inclusion Advisor

10/7/2018

As part of the video series “In Conversation”, Intercross Editor Niki Clark speaks with Jess Markt, ICRC’s Sport and Inclusion Advisor. Injured following a spinal cord accident at age 19, Markt teamed up with the ICRC in 2011 to bring one of America’s best loved-sports, basketball, to wheelchair-bound men and women in Afghanistan, Cambodia, India, South Sudan and elsewhere. In the process, these players, whose once primary identity was ‘disabled’, now see themselves as ‘athletes’ first. In this interview, Markt talks about his position, the role sport plays in helping the disabled recover both physically and emotionally and how sport is bringing joy to countries like Afghanistan after decades of conflict.
base_text:
“In Conversation” with ICRC Sports and Inclusion Advisor

10/7/2018

As part of the video series “In Conversation”, Intercross Editor Niki Clark speaks with Jess Markt, ICRC’s Sport and Inclusion Advisor. Injured following a spinal cord accident at age 19, Markt teamed up with the ICRC in 2011 to bring one of America’s best loved-sports, basketball, to wheelchair-bound men and women in Afghanistan, Cambodia, India, South Sudan and elsewhere. In the process, these players, whose once primary identity was ‘disabled’, now see themselves as ‘athletes’ first. In this interview, Markt talks about his position, the role sport plays in helping the disabled recover both physically and emotionally and how sport is bringing joy to countries like Afghanistan after decades of conflict. **Niki Clark:** Jess, thank you so much for joining us today. To start, could you tell us a little bit about how you became involved with the ICRC and what drew you to using sports, particularly basketball, as a tool for inclusion?

**Jess Markt:** Absolutely, and thanks for having me. My own journey with disability began when I was 19 and sustained a spinal cord injury that left me paralyzed from the chest down. Basketball was always a passion of mine, and discovering wheelchair basketball was transformative—it allowed me to reconnect with a sense of purpose and community. Years later, I learned about the ICRC’s work with people with disabilities, especially in countries affected by conflict, and it struck me that sport could be a powerful way to support their physical and emotional rehabilitation.

As part of the video series “In Conversation”, Intercross Editor Niki Clark speaks with Jess Markt, ICRC’s Sport and Inclusion Advisor. Injured following a spinal cord accident at age 19, Markt teamed up with the ICRC in 2011 to bring one of America’s best loved-sports, basketball, to wheelchair-bound men and women in Afghanistan, Cambodia, India, South Sudan and elsewhere. In the process, these players, whose once primary identity was ‘disabled’, now see themselves as ‘athletes’ first. In this interview, Markt talks about his position, the role sport plays in helping the disabled recover both physically and emotionally aNd how sport is bringing joy to countries like Afghanistan after decades of conflict. **Niki Clark:** Jess, thank you so much for joining us today. To start, could you tell us a little bit about how you became involved with the ICRC and what drew you to using sports, particularly basketball, as a tool for inclusion?

**Jess Markt:** Absolutely, and thanks for having me. My own journey with disability began when I was 19 and sustained a spinal cord injury that left me paralyzed from the chest down. Basketball was always a passion of mine, and discovering wheelchair basketball was transformative—it allowed me to reconnect with a sense of purpose and community. Years later, I learned about the ICRC’s work with people with disabilities, especially in countries affected by conflict, and it struck me that sport could be a powerful way to support their physical and emotional rehabilitation.

analytics (VA) is a sense-making and problem-solving technology that involves diverse analytic, visual, and interactive tools. As problems get increasingly complex, the corresponding techniques and workflows become more and more complex as well. This can lead to situations where users feel lost or do not know how to continue. The analytic progress is stalled. Guidance in visual analytics aims to address this challenge by assisting users in making decisions about how to proceed. Rather than automating the analysis, guidance provides suggestions, highlights potential next steps, and supports users in maintaining analytic momentum. base_text:
Q:

xemacs: dotemacs config so that one can paste without getting "funny" chars

Copying text from websites via browser, paste into xemacs (21.4) buffer, and tildes, quotes, etc. don't copy correctly. Example: he’s a dummy -> he\222s a dummy. Can YOU copy & paste it without problems? This is a common issue when pasting text with "smart quotes" and other Unicode characters into XEmacs, which typically expects ISO-8859-1 (Latin-1) encoding and doesn't handle Unicode by default.

**Solution 1: Use `unify-8859-on-encoding-mode`**

If your XEmacs is built with Mule (multi-lingual extensions), try enabling:

```elisp
(setq-default enable-multibyte-characters t)
(unify-8859-on-encoding-mode 1)
```

Add these lines to your `.emacs` or `.xemacs/init.el` file.

**Solution 2: Replace Smart Quotes After Pasting**

If you still get funny chars, you can map those codes to ASCII equivalents.

prompt_text: Granulocyte chalone assayed in vivo in the mouse. An in vivo system has been developed for estimating the inhibitory activities of granulocyte chalone which overcomes the objection to the use of mixed proliferating target cells.
base_text: Granulocyte chalone assayed in vivo in the mouse. An in vivo system has been developed for estimating the inhibitory activities of granulocyte chalone which overcomes the objection to the use of mixed proliferating target cells. The assay involves the administration of granulocyte chalone preparations to mice, followed by the measurement of DNA synthesis in specifically isolated granulocyte precursor cells. By employing selective labeling techniques and differential cell counting, the system allows for the quantification of chalone-induced inhibition on granulocyte proliferation without interference from other rapidly dividing cell populations. This method provides a sensitive and specific means to evaluate the biological activity of granulocyte chalone in vivo, facilitating further studies on its regulatory role in granulopoiesis.

prompt_text:
The Importance Of Endpoint Protection

As endpoints like laptops, smartphones, tablets and other mobile devices increase, so has the need for endpoint protection. Because the dangers these endpoints pose when they connect to networks are many. Therefore corporates or enterprises have little choice but to use dedicated endpoint protection tools to secure their networks.
base_text:
The Importance of Endpoint Protection

With the proliferation of endpoints such as laptops, smartphones, tablets, and other mobile devices, the necessity for robust endpoint protection has grown significantly. Each of these devices, when connected to a network, introduces a range of potential security risks. As a result, organizations have little choice but to deploy dedicated endpoint protection solutions to safeguard their networks.

Endpoint protection is essential because every device connected to a network serves as a possible entry point for cyber threats, including malware, ransomware, phishing attacks, and unauthorized access. Traditional security measures—such as firewalls and standard antivirus software—are no longer adequate to defend against the increasingly sophisticated and dynamic threats targeting endpoints. Contemporary endpoint protection platforms provide advanced capabilities such as real-time threat detection, automated response, device control, and comprehensive analytics to identify and mitigate risks before they escalate.

Furthermore, the rise of remote work and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) policies has resulted in endpoints frequently operating outside the secure boundaries of corporate networks, thereby heightening the risk of data breaches, information theft, and regulatory non-compliance. Endpoint protection solutions enable organizations to enforce security policies, monitor user activity, and ensure data encryption, regardless of device location.

In conclusion, comprehensive endpoint protection is an indispensable element of any organization’s cybersecurity strategy.

prompt_text: Thyrotrophin-binding inhibitory immunoglobulins and HLA-DRW3-two prognostic factors in Graves' disease. Thyrotrophin-binding inhibitory immuno-globulin (TBII) activity and HLA typing were performed in 38 Chinese patients with Graves' disease to find out the relationship among TBII activity, HLA antigen and relapse. Twelve out of 13 patients who were TBII-positive at anti-thyroid drug withdrawal relapsed. All seven patients with HLA-DR3 antigen relapsed, whereas all ten patients in remission did not have DR3.
base_text: In a study involving 38 Chinese patients with Graves' disease, thyrotrophin-binding inhibitory immunoglobulin (TBII) activity and HLA typing were assessed to explore the relationship among TBII activity, HLA antigen, and relapse. Twelve out of 13 patients who were TBII-positive at the time of anti-thyroid drug withdrawal experienced relapse. Similarly, all seven patients carrying the HLA-DR3 antigen relapsed, while none of the ten patients in remission possessed DR3. The combination of persistent TBII activity and the presence of HLA-DR3 antigen thus appears to be strongly associated with an increased risk of relapse after discontinuation of anti-thyroid therapy. Conversely, patients who were TBII-negative and did not have the HLA-DR3 antigen showed a significantly lower rate of relapse.
final_text: In a study involving 38 Chinese patients with Graves' disease, thyrotrophin-binding inhibitory immunoglobulin (TBII) activity and HLA typing were assessed to explore the relationship among TBII activity, HLA antigen, and relapse. Twelve out of 13 patients who were TBII-positive at the time of anti-thyroid drug withdrawal experienced relapse. Similarly, all seven patients carrying the HLA-DR3 antigen relapsed, while none of the ten patients in remission possessed DR3. The combination of persistent TBII activity and the presence of HLA-DR3 antigen thus appears to be strongly associated with an increased risk of relapse after discontinuation of anti-thyroid therapy. Conversely, patients who were TBII-negative and did not have the HLA-DR3 antigen showed a significantly lower rate of relapse.

Rhodium acetylacetonate

Rhodium acetylacetonate is the coordination complex with the formula Rh(O2C5H7)3, which is sometimes known as Rh(acac)3. The molecule has D3-symmetry. It is a yellow-orange solid that is soluble in organic solvents.
base_text: Rhodium acetylacetonate is a coordination complex with the formula Rh(O₂C₅H₇)₃, commonly referred to as Rh(acac)₃. The molecule exhibits D₃ symmetry and appears as a yellow-orange solid that is soluble in organic solvents. Similar to other metal acetylacetonates, Rh(acac)₃ serves as a precursor for the synthesis of various rhodium complexes and functions as a catalyst or catalyst precursor in a range of organic transformations. Its notable thermal stability and solubility render it particularly suitable for applications in homogeneous catalysis, especially in hydrogenation and hydroformylation reactions. The complex is typically synthesized by reacting rhodium(III) chloride with acetylacetone in the presence of a base.
final_text: Rhodium acetylacetonate is a coordination complex with the formula Rh(O₂C₅H₇)₃, commonly referred to as Rh(acac)₃. The molecule exhibits D₃ symmetry and appears as a yellow-orange solid that is soluble in organic solvents. Similar to other metal acetylacetonates, Rh(acac)₃ serves as a precursor foe the synthesis of various rhodium complexes and functions as a catalyst or catalyst precursor in a range of organic transformations. Its notable thermal stability and solubility render it particularly suitable for applications in homogeneous catalysis, especially in hydrogenation and hydroformylation reactions. The complex is typically synthesized by reacting rhodium(III) chloride with acetylacetone in the presence of a base.

base_text:
Saturday, November 25, 2006

I wonder when the White House will finally bow to reality and acknowledge that what what's happening in Iraq is in fact civil war. The administration's terminology is slow to evolve, and tends to change only when it's been lapped by actual events. Remember how long Rumsfeld and company refused to say the word "insurgency?" "Civil war" will be an even harder pill to swallow -- or phrase to cough out -- because as soon as America accepts that Iraq is in a civil war, the voters will conclude the situation is hopeless, none of our business, and not worth any more American blood and treasure. But at some point, language loses its power to mask the truth, and the facts on the ground become undeniable. Sectarian militias openly control neighborhoods, death squads roam at night, and the elected government is powerless to stop the violence. To keep calling this anything but a civil war is not just disingenuous—it’s an insult to the public’s intelligence. The administration might think it can buy time or avoid blame by sticking to euphemisms, but in the end, reality has a way of asserting itself.

What’s disturbing is that the debate over language has real consequences. By refusing to call it a civil war, the White House avoids the hard choices such an admission would demand: rethinking strategy, considering withdrawal, or committing to a much larger intervention. Instead, they seem content to manage perceptions, as if managing perceptions could somehow turn the tide on the ground.

prompt_text:
Q:

How to get encrypted password from database to show (still as::password) on update/edit? I am able to add new users in ActiveAdmin.
base_text:
Q:

How to get encrypted password from database to show (still as::password) on update/edit? I am able to add new users in ActiveAdmin. In ActiveAdmin (and Rails in general), it is not possible—and not secure—to retrieve the original plaintext password once it’s been encrypted (hashed) and stored in the database. Passwords are typically hashed using a one-way algorithm (like bcrypt), meaning the original password can't be recovered or displayed.

**Best Practice:**

- On the user edit form, **do not pre-fill** the password field.
- If you leave the password fields blank and submit, the password remains unchanged.
- If you enter a new password, it will update.

**How to handle this in ActiveAdmin:**

```ruby
# In your ActiveAdmin User form:
form do |f|
  f.inputs do
    f.input :email
    f.input :password
    f.input :password_confirmation
    # Other fields
  end
  f.actions
end
```

**Explanation:**

- The password fields are empty when editing.
- User must enter a new password if they want to change it.
final_text:
Q:

In ActiveAdmin (and Rails in general), it is not possible—and not secure—to retrieve the original plaintext password once it’s been encrypted (hashed) and stored in the database. Passwords are typically hashed using a one-way algorithm (like bcrypt), meaning the original password can't be recovered or displayed.

**Best Practice:**

- If you leave the password fields blank and submit, the password remains unchanged.
- If you enter a new password, it will update.

**How to handle this in ActiveAdmin:**


Q:

Why is ToLowerCase() giving a type error in Express js / Node.js? req.body.email.ToLowerCase() gives an error in Expressjs. Do we need to require something to use it? A:

If the email from the body is a string you can use the method toLowerCase();.
base_text:
Q:

Why is ToLowerCase() giving a type error in Express js / Node.js? req.body.email.ToLowerCase() gives an error in Expressjs. Do we need to require something to use it? A:

If the email from the body is a string you can use the method toLowerCase();. However, JavaScript is case-sensitive, and the correct method name is `toLowerCase()` (with a lowercase "t"). If you use `ToLowerCase()`, you'll get a TypeError because that method does not exist.

Also, make sure that `req.body.email` is actually a string. If it's `undefined` or not a string, you'll also get a TypeError.
final_text:
Q:

Why is ToLowerCase() giving a type error in Express js / Node.js? req.body.email.ToLowerCase() gives an error in Expressjs. Do we need to require something to use it? A:

If the email from the body is a string you can use the method toLowerCase();. However, JavaScript is case-sensitive, and the correct method name is `toLowerCase()` (with a lowercase "t"). If you use `ToLowerCase()`, you'll get a TypeError because that metHod does not exist.

Also, make sare that `req.body.email` is actually a string. If it's `undefined` or not a string, you'll also get a TypeError.

Every two seconds in the United States, a child suffers a nonfatal injury, and around 30,000 children lose their lives each year due to injury-related incidents. Recognizing the seriousness of this issue, the U.S. Department of Health has made reducing unintentional injuries a top public health priority. However, injury prevention programs frequently fail to address the specific challenges and viewpoints of adolescent mothers. This focus group study examined how young mothers understand their parenting responsibilities and their role in safeguarding their children from injuries. While participants voiced genuine concern for their children’s well-being, many were unfamiliar with typical household dangers or effective ways to prevent accidents. Instead of turning to formal educational materials, they often depended on their own experiences, advice from relatives, or information found on social media.

Obstacles to adopting injury prevention measures included financial constraints, unstable living situations, and a lack of reliable social networks. Additionally, some mothers described feeling judged or disregarded by healthcare professionals, which made them hesitant to seek guidance or support.

Recognizing the seriousness of this issue, the U.S. Department of Health has made reducIng unintentional inuries a top public health priority. However, injury prevention programs frequently fail to address the specific challenges and viewpoints of adolescent mothers. This focus group study examined how young mothers understand their parenting responsibilities and their role in safeguarding their children from injuries. While participants voiced genuine concern for their children’s well-being, many were unfamiliar with typical household dangers or effective ways to prevent accidents. Instead of turning to formal educational materials, they often depended on their own experiences, advice from relatives, or information found on social media.

Obstacles to adopting injury prevention measures included financial constraints, unstable living situations, and a lack of reliable social networks. Additionally, some mothers described feeling judged or disregarded by healthcare professionals, which made them hesitant to seek guidance or support.

Q:

Unable to draw bitmaps on top of other bitmap using Direct2D

I want to write a Direct2D application that draws many objects on a background image using SharpDX. But after drawing a certain number of objects, Direct2D ignores all further objects.
base_text:
I want to write a Direct2D application that draws many objects on a background image using SharpDX. However, after drawing a certain number of objects, Direct2D stops rendering any further objects. This issue is often caused by reaching the maximum number of bitmap resources or exceeding GPU memory limits. In Direct2D (and through SharpDX), each time you create a new `Bitmap` or `BitmapRenderTarget`, it uses GPU resources. If you create too many bitmaps without releasing them, you can hit resource limits, and Direct2D may silently fail to draw new bitmaps.

**Common causes and solutions:**

1. **Resource Leaks:**  
   Make sure you are disposing of all `Bitmap` and `BitmapRenderTarget` objects when they are no longer needed. In C#, use `using` statements or explicitly call `.Dispose()`.
final_text: I want to write a Direct2D application that draws many objects on a background image using SharpDX. However, after drawing a certain number of objects, Direct2D stops rendering any further objects. This issue is often caused by reaching the maximum number of bitmap resources or exceeding GPU memory limits. In Direct2D ( and through SharpDX ), each time you create a new ` ` or ` BitmapRenderTarget `, it uses GPU resources. If you create too many bitmaps without releasing them, you can hit resource limits, and Direct2D may silently fail to draw new bitmaps. * * Common causes and solutions: * * 1. * * Resource Leaks: * * Make sure you are disposing of all ` Bitmap ` and ` BitmapRenderTarget ` objects when they are no longer needed. In C #, use ` using ` statements or explicitly call `.Dispose ( ) `.
