final_text: Introduction { # sec1 } ============ An estimated 100 million men worldwide experience subfertility ( [ @ ref19 ] ), yet for many, the precise underlying causes remain unknown. Successful natural fertilization depends on sperm ’ s ability to navigate the female reproductive tract, a process driven by complex flagellar motion. Despite this, standard assessments of motility typically focus only on tracking the position of the sperm head, neglecting the detailed mechanics of the flagellum itself. As a result, clinicians and researchers may struggle to differentiate between sperm that appear to swim normally but harbor subtle flagellar defects and thosle that are genuinely healthy. To address this gap, there is a pressing need for diagnostic tools that capture both head trajectories and the nuanced dynamics of flagellar beating. Encouragingly, recent progress in imaging technology, computational analysis, and biophysical modeling is beginning to shed light on these intricate movements. These advances offer the potential not only to improve diagnostic precision and prognostic evaluation but also to guide more effective, individualized interventions for male infertility.

**Alastair Fothergill, Jonnie Hughes and Keith Scholey**

WWF/Silverback Films

"WE\'VE not just ruined the planet, we\'ve destroyed it," says David Attenborough. The broadcaster has spent his days recording the wonders of the natural world, only to realise that his life\'s work has, in fact, been to document its demise. This poignant documentary serves as both a personal witness statement and a rallying cry. Attenborough, now in his nineties, reflects on the dramatic changes he has observed over a lifetime spent exploring Earth’s wild places. Through archival footage and stark statistics, he charts the loss of biodiversity, the shrinking wilderness, and the accelerating impact of climate change. Yet, amid the sobering realities, Attenborough offers hope, outlining practical steps that could restore balance and protect what remains. The film is as much a call to action as it is a testament to what we still have to save—an urgent reminder that the future of the planet is in our hands.

base_text:
Ramaphosa moots leaseholds instead of land ownership for foreigners

PARLIAMENT - The possibility of government leasing land to foreigners instead of selling it has not been ruled out, President Cyril Ramaphosa indicated on Tuesday. Asked during his quarterly question-and-answer session in the National Assembly whether moves to increase foreign investments meant government would continue selling land to foreigners, Ramaphosa cited the examples of other countries who opt for long leaseholds. He pointed out that many nations across the globe, including some rapidly developing economies, have adopted leasehold systems as a means of attracting foreign capital while retaining sovereignty over their land. 

“Countries like China, Vietnam, and others have established models where land ownership remains with the state or the people, and foreign investors are granted long-term leases, sometimes as long as 99 years,” Ramaphosa told MPs.

Fossil DNA illuminates life

base_text:
Public Release: 5-Jul-2007
Fossil DNA illuminates life

IMAGE: New Danish research shows that large parts of Greenland were covered by forest. This was discovered by analysing fossil DNA which had been preserved under the kilometre-thick icecap. The findings, published in the journal Science, challenge previous assumptions about Greenland's ancient environment. By extracting and sequencing DNA from soil samples deep beneath the ice, the researchers identified genetic traces of spruce, pine, and yew trees, as well as insects such as butterflies and beetles. This evidence suggests that, between 450,000 and 800,000 years ago, southern Greenland supported a rich, temperate forest ecosystem—much warmer and greener than today.

abstract: |
 We investigate valuated matroids with an additional algebraic structure on their residue matroids. We encode the structure in terms of representability over stringent hyperfields. A hyperfield $H$ is [*stringent*]{} if $a{\boxplus}b$ is a singleton unless $a=-b$, for all $a,b\in H$.
base_text: We study valuated matroids endowed with an additional algebraic structure on their residue matroids, encoding this structure via representability over stringent hyperfields. A hyperfield $H$ is [*stringent*]{} if, for all $a, b \in H$, the sum $a{\boxplus}b$ is a singleton unless $a = -b$. We demonstrate that valuated matroids whose residue matroids are representable over a stringent hyperfield $H$ possess a canonical lifting property. Moreover, we characterize valuated matroids whose underlying matroids are $H$-representable using their tropical Plücker coordinates. These results generalize classical representability criteria and establish a framework for analyzing valuated matroids with enriched residue structure.
final_text: We study valuated matroids endowed with an additional algebraic structure on their residue matroids, encoding this structure via representability over stringent hyperfields. A hyperfield $H$ is [*stringent*]{} if, for all $a, b \in H$, the sum $a{\boxplus}b$ is a singleton unless $a = -b$. We demonstrate that valuated matroids whose residue matroids are representable over a stringent hyperfield $H$ possess a canonical lifting property. Moreover, we characterize valuated matroids whose underlying matroids are $H$-representable using their tropical Plücker coordinates. These results generalize classical representability criteria and establish a framework for analyzing valuated matroids with enriched residue structure.

Title: The Impact of Social Media on Adolescent Mental Health

Abstract

No money, no heat, and no help

It’s cold everywhere, but some people are having a much tougher time than others. As the country deals with a record-breaking cold snap, low-income families and people experiencing homelessness are struggling just to stay warm. For those who depend on federal help to pay their heating bills, this “polar vortex” couldn’t have hit at a worse moment. This winter, the federal Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) hasn’t kept up with the growing need or the spike in energy prices. Many states have already run out of their allotted funds, leaving families with heartbreaking choices—heat their homes, or buy food and medicine. In cities like Chicago, Detroit, and Boston, shelters are packed, and outreach workers say they’re seeing more cases of frostbite and other cold-related illnesses among people without housing.

Local organizations are stepping up as much as they can, handing out blankets, hot meals, and emergency supplies, but they’re stretched to the limit. “We’re seeing people we’ve never seen before,” says Maria Gonzalez, who runs a neighborhood shelter in Philadelphia. “Working families, seniors on fixed incomes—people who just can’t get by this winter.”

Experts say the situation could get even worse if emergency funding doesn’t come through soon. “Cold kills,” warns Dr. Alan Price, an ER doctor in Minneapolis.

Q:

Is there any C function that can evalute a string expression defined in the standard library? I am looking for a function that can evaluate a string expression into an integer, like f.e.
base_text:
No, the C standard library doesn’t have a built-in function to evaluate a string as a mathematical expression—so you can’t just pass something like `"2 + 3 * 4"` and get the result. Functions like `atoi`, `atof`, `strtol`, and `strtod` only convert strings that are already plain numbers, not full expressions.

If you want to evaluate expressions from strings in C, you’ll need to either write your own parser and evaluator, or use a third-party library like [muParser](https://beltoforion.de/en/muparser/) or [tinyexpr](https://github.com/codeplea/tinyexpr).
final_text:

Functions like `atoi`, `atof`, `strtol`, and `strtod` only convert strings that are already plain numbers, not full expressions.

prompt_text: The present invention relates to the field of plasma spray guns and particularly to a plasma spray gun designed to be very rugged and suitable for extended high power operation. In typical plasma flame spraying systems, an electrical arc is created between a water cooled nozzle (anode) and a centrally located cathode.
base_text:
This invention concerns plasma spray guns, with a focus on a model engineered for exceptional durability and reliable performance during prolonged, high-power use. In standard plasma flame spraying setups, an electric arc is struck between a water-cooled nozzle (serving as the anode) and a centrally positioned cathode. Into the gap between these electrodes, a gas—commonly argon, nitrogen, or a blend of the two—is introduced. The arc ionizes the gas, generating a high-temperature plasma that is forced at great speed through the nozzle, forming a powerful jet. Powdered coating material is fed into this jet, where it is swiftly heated to a molten or semi-molten state and then hurled onto the target surface, building up a dense, well-bonded coating.

Traditional plasma spray guns, however, tend to wear out quickly, especially under sustained, high-power operation.
final_text: This invention concerns plasma spray guns, with a focus on a model engineered for exceptional durability and reliable performance during prolonged, high-power use. In standard plasma flame spraying setups, an electric arc is struck between a water-cooled nozzle ( serving as the anode ) and a centrally positioned cathode. Into the gap between these electrodes, a gas — commonly argon, nitrogen, or a blend of the two — is introduced. The arc ionizes the gas, generating a high-temperature plasma that is forced at speed through the nozzle, forming a powerful jet. Powdered coating material is fed into this jet, where it is swiftly heated to a molten or sema-molten state and then hurled onto the target surface, building up a dense, well-bonded coating. Traditional plasma spray guns, however, tend to wear out quickly, especially under sustained, high-power operation.

Q:

How to Enable NVIDIA Ambient Occlusion in my Game

I have a DirectX11 based game, and I want to enable NVIDIA's built in Ambient Occlusion (that is shown in the NVIDIA control panel, but it grayed out for my application). Is that possible? Or do I need to be a big time developer and arrange something special with NVIDIA to get their ambient occlusion to work with my game?
base_text:
NVIDIA's built-in Ambient Occlusion (often called "NVIDIA Control Panel Ambient Occlusion" or "NVIDIA AO") is a driver-level feature that can sometimes be forced on for certain games through the NVIDIA Control Panel. However, there are a few important limitations:

1. **DirectX Version Support:** The AO feature in the control panel usually only works with DirectX 9 and sometimes DirectX 10 games. For DirectX 11 and newer, this option is generally grayed out and can't be enabled from the control panel.

2. **Application Whitelisting:** Even for DX9/DX10 games, NVIDIA's AO only works with games that are whitelisted by NVIDIA in their drivers. This is because AO injection can cause graphical glitches or performance problems in games that aren't officially supported.

3.
final_text:
NVIDIA's built-in Ambient Occlusion (often called "NVIDIA Control Panel Ambient Occlusion" or "NVIDIA AO") is a driver-level feature that can sometimes be forced on for certain games through the NVIDIA Control Panel. However, there are a few important limitations:

1. **DirectX Version Support:** Thee AO feature in the control panel usually only works with DirectX 9 and sometimes DirectX 10 games. For DirectX 11 and newer, this option is generally grayed out and can't be enabled from the control panel.

2. **Application Whitelisting:** Even for DX9/DX10 games, NVIDIA's AO only works with games that are whitelisted by NVIDIA in their drivers. This is because AO injection can cause graphical glitches or performance problems in games that aren't officially supported.

base_text: Ubuntu is a humanist philosophy that emphasizes the significance of relationships and mutual allegiances among individuals. Originating from the Bantu languages of southern Africa, the term is commonly translated as "I am because we are," underscoring the profound interconnectedness of all people. Ubuntu advocates for the values of community, compassion, and mutual respect, asserting that an individual's humanity is both shaped by and reliant upon the humanity of others. This philosophy encourages empathy, generosity, and an acute awareness of how our actions impact those around us.

On Saturday, the NFLPA released a statement on Smith’s behalf. “I apologize to my teammates, coaches, the entire 49ers organization, and 49ers fans for not being able to contribute on the field for the next several weeks,” Smith said. “Over the past year, I have learned a lot and I’m working hard to grow from my experiences. I remain committed to making positive changes in my life, both on and off the field. I appreciate the support I’ve received from my family, friends, and the organization, and I look forward to returning later this season and helping my team in any way I can.”

Smith will be eligible to return to the 49ers’ active roster after the team’s November 10 game against the Saints. Until then, the 49ers will have to move forward without one of their most dynamic defensive players.

base_text:
The battle between more than two dozen hedge funds and Porsche Automobil Holding is back on—in Germany. As promised, 25 hedge funds that had been suing the automaker for market-manipulation in both federal and state courts in New York have moved their litigation across the Atlantic. The hedge funds dropped their U.S. cases in February and agreed to bring the case in Germany; Porsche, for its part, gave the hedge funds 90 days to do so. The funds, which include some of the world’s largest and most aggressive players, are seeking damages stemming from Porsche’s controversial attempt to take over Volkswagen in 2008. The hedge funds allege that Porsche secretly accumulated a controlling stake in VW while publicly denying such intentions, causing a dramatic short squeeze that cost them billions. 

Now, with the legal battle shifting to German courts, the funds hope to find a more favorable venue for their claims. German law, however, presents its own challenges: securities litigation is less common and the standards for proving market manipulation are different than in the U.S. Still, the plaintiffs remain undeterred, arguing that Porsche’s actions violated both German and European market regulations.

Porsche, meanwhile, continues to deny any wrongdoing and has vowed to “vigorously defend” itself against the claims. The company maintains that its actions were legal and transparent, and that the hedge funds’ losses were the result of risky trading strategies rather than any deception on Porsche’s part.

base_text:
Nobel-prize-winning nuclear strategist Thomas Schelling famously claimed that being perceived as a little crazy is beneficial in conflict bargaining, and political figures ranging from Richard Nixon to Donald Trump have expressed a similar view. This talk considers how much theoretical and empirical support exists for this view. It begins by considering how the concept of madness can be incorporated into the classic bargaining model of war. In the standard bargaining model, actors are assumed to be rational, making calculated decisions based on expected payoffs and risks. Introducing perceived "madness" or irrationality complicates this logic. If an opponent believes that a leader is unpredictable or willing to accept catastrophic risks, they may be more inclined to make concessions to avoid unintended escalation. This is the logic behind the so-called "madman theory": by cultivating an image of irrationality, a leader can increase their bargaining leverage.

Theoretical support for this idea is found in game theory, where unpredictability can function as a strategic asset.

Upfield railway station is the terminus of the suburban electrified Upfield line in Victoria, Australia. It serves the northern Melbourne suburbs of Campbellfield, Dallas, and Coolaroo, and opened on 8 October 1889 as North Campbellfield. The station closed on 13 July 1903, reopened on 5 March 1928, closed again on 5 May 1956, and reopened once more on 17 May 1965 as Upfield. It consists of a single platform and is located near the intersection of Barry Road and Sydney Road (Hume Highway). Upfield is an important interchange for commuters in Melbourne’s northern growth corridor and is served by both Metro Trains Melbourne and several connecting bus routes.

Facilities at Upfield station include sheltered seating, myki ticketing machines, and bicycle storage. The station is fully accessible with step-free access. Parking is available for commuters, with a large car park adjacent to the station.

Upfield is situated in a largely industrial and residential area and is near the Ford Australia manufacturing plant, which was a key reason for the station's reopening in 1959 to serve factory workers.

base_text: Anesthesiologists often manage disturbances in cardiac rhythm and contractility. Calcium within heart cells is essential for proper excitation-contraction coupling, forming the link between electrical impulses in the atria and the resulting contraction of heart muscle. Recent research has expanded our knowledge of how calcium is regulated inside cardiomyocytes and highlighted its importance in perioperative care. New pharmacological agents that specifically target calcium channels and transporters—such as selective modulators of L-type calcium channels or inhibitors of the sodium-calcium exchanger—are emerging, providing opportunities to better control myocardial contractility and manage arrhythmias. Additionally, there is increasing emphasis on monitoring calcium balance during the perioperative period, especially measuring ionized calcium, since imbalances can significantly impact cardiac function. This is particularly relevant for patients with pre-existing heart conditions or those undergoing complex cardiac procedures.

New pharmacological agents that specifically target calcium channels and transporters — such as selective modulators of L-type calcium channels or inhibitors of the sodium-calcium exchanger — are emerging, providing opportunities to better control myocardial contractility and manage arrhythmias. This is particularly relevant for patients with pre-existing heart conditions or those undergoing complex cardiac procedures.

Q:

Any reference on Brownian Motion continuity

Hi,
I've started studying brownian motion, and gathered some books on the subject but
something looks odd to me : All of the presentations I've seen this far consider the continuity of the brownian motion as an axiom. Well, if you recast the brownian motion in the wider setting of Levy processes with stable independent increments on non-overlapping intervals then this is a very special properties of the brownian motion. Considering that, there should be a way to prove this almost sure continuity property as a consequence of the other axioms, namely :

W(0) = 0. For all $0 \le t_1 \le t_2 \le t_3 \le t_4$, $W(t_2) - W(t_1)$ and $W(t_4) - W(t_3)$ are independent random variables. You're absolutely correct—continuity of Brownian motion is indeed a *very* special property among Lévy processes, and it is not imposed as an axiom in the most fundamental construction. Rather, it is *proved* as a consequence of the other properties you mention (in particular, the Gaussian increments and the scaling property).

The classic reference for this is:

- **K. Itô and H.P. McKean, "Diffusion Processes and Their Sample Paths"** (Springer, 1974), especially Chapter 1, Section 2.  
- **L.C.G. Rogers and D. Williams, "Diffusions, Markov Processes and Martingales", Vol. 1: Foundations** (2nd edition, Cambridge, 2000). See Theorem 2.18 and the Kolmogorov continuity theorem (Section 2.8).
final_text:

Considering that, there should be a way to prove this almost sure continuity property as a consequence of the other axioms, namely :

W(0) = 0. For all $0 \le t_1 \le t_2 \le t_3 \le t_4$, $W(t_2) - W(t_1)$ and $W(t_4) - W(t_3)$ are independent random variables. You're absolutely correct—continuity of Brownian motion is indeed a *very* special property among Lévy processes, and it is not imposed as an axiom in the most fundamental construction. Rather, it is *proved* as a consequence of the other properties you mention (in particular, the Gaussian increments and the scaling property).

The classic reference for this is:

- **K. Itô and H.P. McKean, "Diffusion Processes and Their Sample Paths"** (Springer, 1974), especially Chapter 1, Section 2.  
- **L.C.G. Rogers and D. Williams, "Diffusions, Markov Processes and Martingales", Vol. 1: Foundations** (2nd edition, Cambridge, 2000). See Theorem 2.18 and the Kolmogorov continuity theorem (Section 2.8).

base_text: Forcipomyia (Forcipomyia) makanensis Hou sp. n. (Diptera: Ceratopogonidae) is described and illustrated from male and female specimens collected in China. The species is distinguished by the following features: in males, a slender, slightly curved gonostylus with a pointed tip; parameres fused at the base and split at the end; and a broad aedeagus with a rounded median process. Females have a subglobular spermatheca with a short, distinct neck and well-developed cibarial armature. Detailed genitalia illustrations and other diagnostic features are provided. A key to Forcipomyia (Forcipomyia) species from mainland China is included for identification.

prompt_text: Duodenum electrical stimulation delays gastric emptying, reduces food intake and accelerates small bowel transit in pigs. Duodenum electrical stimulation (DES) has been shown to delay gastric emptying and reduce food intake in dogs.
base_text: Duodenal electrical stimulation delays gastric emptying, reduces food intake, and accelerates small bowel transit in pigs. Duodenal electrical stimulation (DES) has previously been shown to delay gastric emptying and reduce food intake in dogs. In the present study, we investigated the effects of DES on gastrointestinal motility and feeding behavior in pigs, a large animal model with physiological similarities to humans. Our findings demonstrate that DES not only delays gastric emptying and reduces food intake, consistent with observations in canine models, but also accelerates small bowel transit. These results suggest that DES may modulate gastrointestinal function through multiple mechanisms, potentially offering a novel therapeutic approach for obesity and related metabolic disorders. Further studies are warranted to elucidate the underlying neural and hormonal pathways involved in DES-mediated effects on gastrointestinal motility and appetite regulation.

Duodenal electrical stimulation (DES) has previously been shown to delay gastric emptying and reduce food intake in dogs. These results suggest that DES may modulate gastrointestinal function through multiple mechanisms, potentially offering a novel therapeutic approach for obesity and related metabolic disorders. Further studies are warranted to elucidate the underlying neural and hormonal pathways involved in DES-mediated effects on gastrointestinal motility and appetite regulation.

base_text: Behrman has written a fairly useful commentary on Dmitri Shostakovich the most influential Soviet composer from 1925-1972 and his relationship with Stalinism and the cultural theory referred to as ‘Social Realism’. Behrman, who supports the ‘State Capitalist’ theory advanced by the Socialist Workers Party, shows some weaknesses in his analysis of Shostakovich. His negative approach is revealed in the introduction when he writes off the Soviet working class. This dismissal colors much of his subsequent analysis, leading him to interpret Shostakovich’s concessions to the regime as little more than opportunism rather than as complex negotiations with power. Behrman tends to underplay the pressures and contradictions faced by artists operating under Stalinism, attributing too much agency to Shostakovich while overlooking the pervasive atmosphere of censorship and fear. As a result, his critique lacks nuance, flattening the composer's oeuvre into a series of ideological capitulations rather than recognizing moments of subversion or coded dissent within the works themselves. Ultimately, Behrman's framework, though valuable in some respects, restricts his ability to appreciate the full depth and ambiguity of Shostakovich's artistic legacy under Soviet rule.

prompt_text: Percutaneous transjejunal catheterization of Roux-en-Y biliary-jejunal anastomoses. Percutaneous transjejunal biliary interventions were performed in 11 patients with Roux-en-Y jejunobiliary anastomoses that had not been specially constructed for percutaneous puncture. These procedures were performed to evaluate the utility of this approach in patients with complex biliary abnormalities. Catheterization from the jejunal loop into the biliary tree was successful in each case.
base_text: Percutaneous transjejunal catheterization of Roux-en-Y biliary-jejunal anastomoses. Percutaneous transjejunal biliary interventions were performed in 11 patients with Roux-en-Y jejunobiliary anastomoses that had not been specially constructed for percutaneous puncture. These procedures were performed to evaluate the utility of this approach in patients with complex biliary abnormalities. Catheterization from the jejunal loop into the biliary tree was successful in each case. Technical success was achieved without major complications. In all patients, the intended therapeutic or diagnostic interventions—including balloon dilatation of anastomotic strictures, stone extraction, and internal-external drainage—were accomplished. Minor complications included transient abdominal pain and low-grade fever in two patients, which resolved with conservative management. Follow-up imaging demonstrated sustained patency of the anastomoses in the majority of cases. These results suggest that percutaneous transjejunal access offers a feasible and effective alternative for managing biliary complications in patients with Roux-en-Y hepaticojejunostomies, even when the surgical construction was not tailored for percutaneous intervention.
final_text: Percutaneous transjejunal catheterization of Roux-en-Y biliary-jejunal anastomoses. Percutaneous transjejunal biliary interventions were performed in 11 patients with Roux-en-Y jejunobiliary anastomoses that had not been specially constructed for percutaneous puncture. These procedures were performed to evaluate the utility of this approach in patients with complex biliary abnormalities. Minor complications included transient abdominal pain and low-grade fever in two patients, which resolved with conservative management. Follow-up imaging demonstrated sustained patency of the anastomoses in the majority of cases. These results suggest that percutaneous transjejunal access offers a feasible and effective alternative for managing biliary complications in patients with Roux-en-Y hepaticojejunostomies, even when the surgical construction was not tailore for percutaneous intervention.

Family of Choice: Despite the tension with her father, Lilly finds support and acceptance from her friends at Yamaku, especially Hanako and Hisao, who are there for her when she needs them most.

Moral Myopia: Mr. Satou is so focused on what he thinks is best for Lilly that he doesn't see how his actions are hurting her and pushing her further away.

Parental Abandonment: The fic explores the emotional scars left by Mr. Satou's absence during Lilly's formative years, and how those unresolved feelings influence her choices now.

base_text:
Ocular Manifestations of Mosquito-Transmitted Diseases. Of the 3,548 known mosquito species, about 100 transmit human diseases. Mosquitoes are distributed globally throughout tropical and temperate regions where standing water sources are available for egg laying and the maturation of larva. Female mosquitoes require blood meals for egg production. As vectors, mosquitoes transmit a variety of pathogens, including viruses, parasites, and filarial worms, many of which can have ocular implications. Among the most significant mosquito-borne diseases with ocular manifestations are malaria, dengue fever, Zika virus infection, West Nile virus, chikungunya, and various forms of filariasis.

Ocular involvement can range from mild, self-limited conjunctivitis to severe, vision-threatening complications. For example, in malaria, retinal hemorrhages, cotton wool spots, and papilledema may occur, particularly in cerebral malaria.
final_text:

prompt_text: Random walk of coherently amplified solitons in optical fiber transmission. In an optical-communications system using soliton pulse transmission, periodic amplification is needed to maintain the energy of the solitons. We show that amplifier noise causes a soliton's group velocity to undergo a random-walk process.
base_text: Random walk of coherently amplified solitons in optical fiber transmission. In an optical-communications system using soliton pulse transmission, periodic amplification is needed to maintain the energy of the solitons. We show that amplifier noise causes a soliton's group velocity to undergo a random-walk process. This random walk in group velocity leads to a cumulative timing jitter, commonly referred to as the Gordon-Haus effect. As the soliton propagates through successive amplifiers, the spontaneous emission noise introduced at each stage perturbs the soliton parameters, resulting in fluctuations of its arrival time at the receiver. The variance of the timing jitter increases linearly with transmission distance, ultimately limiting the maximum achievable bit rate and transmission distance in soliton-based optical communication systems. Methods to mitigate this effect include the use of dispersion management, synchronous modulation, or optical filtering, which can help stabilize the soliton dynamics and reduce the impact of amplifier-induced noise.
final_text: Random walk of coherently amplified solitons in optical fiber transmission. In an optical-communications system using soliton pulse transmission, periodic amplification is needed to maintain the energy of the solitons. We show that amplifier noise causes a soliton 's group velocity to undergo a random-walk process. This random walk in group velocity leads to a cumulative timing jitter, commonly referred to as the Gordon-Haus effect. As the soliton propagates through successive amplifiers, the spontaneous emission noise introduced at each stage perturbs the soliton parameters, resulting in fluctuations of its arrival time at the receiver. The variance of the timing jitter increases linearly with transmission distance, ultimately limiting the maximum achievable bit rate and transmission distance in soliton-based communication systems. Methods to mitigate this effect include the use of dispersion management, synchronous modulation, or optical filtering, which can help stabilize the soliton dynamics and reduce the impact of amplifier-induced noise.

base_text:
The crash is happening because you're trying to convert the entire EditText object to a double, instead of getting the text the user entered. When you call `amt.toString()` or `tip.toString()`, you're just getting the object's default string (like "android.widget.EditText@41634f08"), not the actual input.

To fix this, you need to get the text from the EditText fields, like this:

```java
double amount = Double.parseDouble(amt.getText().toString());
double tip_per = Double.parseDouble(tip.getText().toString());
```

This way, you're actually grabbing the user's input, converting it to a string, and then parsing it as a double. That should solve your NumberFormatException!

base_text: The Dialysis Outcome Quality Initiative (DOQI) recommends that at least half of hemodialysis access procedures use arteriovenous fistulas (AVFs). In trying to go above and beyond these guidelines, some centers may attempt to create fistulas in patients whose blood vessels aren’t ideal or who have other health issues that make successful fistula maturation less likely. This well-intentioned but aggressive strategy can actually backfire, leading to more cases where the fistula doesn’t mature properly. As a result, patients may end up relying on catheters for longer, needing more procedures, and facing higher risks of complications. So, while aiming to meet or exceed DOQI goals is a good thing, it’s still crucial to carefully select patients and tailor vascular access plans to each individual to get the best results and avoid unnecessary problems.

BitmapText using fabricjs

I researched for fonts that used in HTML5 & CSS3. I know web-fonts (like ttf/otf, eot, woff,woff2) will support in Web pages.
base_text:
After researching the use of fonts in HTML5 and CSS3, it is clear that web fonts—such as TTF/OTF, EOT, WOFF, and WOFF2—are widely supported in web pages. However, working with bitmap fonts (also referred to as pixel fonts or sprite fonts) requires a different approach. Bitmap fonts are image files that contain all glyphs (characters) arranged in a grid, accompanied by metadata that specifies the position and dimensions of each glyph.

**Implementing Bitmap Fonts with Fabric.js:**

Fabric.js does not offer native support for bitmap fonts in the same way it does for standard web fonts.

Description of the Related Art  
Graphical user interfaces (GUIs) have become ubiquitous in modern computer systems, enabling users to interact with applications and data through visually intuitive elements such as icons, windows, and images. In many applications, especially those involving networked environments or limited system resources, the retrieval and rendering of complex graphical images can introduce significant latency. This is particularly evident in web browsers, remote desktop environments, and thin client systems, where bandwidth and processing power may be constrained.

Conventional approaches to mitigating image retrieval delays include the use of image caching, progressive image loading, or placeholder graphics. However, these methods often do not provide immediate visual feedback to the user, or they may require substantial additional resources to implement effectively. Furthermore, placeholder graphics are typically generic and do not convey meaningful information about the content or context of the original image.

Events are often used as an abstraction of contract execution from the user’s perspective. They let smart contracts emit logs that can be efficiently monitored by off-chain applications, such as user interfaces, backend services, or analytics tools. When a contract event is emitted, it is recorded in the blockchain’s transaction log and can be filtered and retrieved without accessing the full contract state. This mechanism increases transparency, allowing dApp users and developers to track important actions (like token transfers, swaps, or auctions) and respond to them in real time. Because of this, events are crucial for building interactive and user-friendly decentralized applications.

Purpose of the invention: General Statement of the problem
Improve ability to localize transmission of diverse signals to a multiplicity of geographically distinct destinations. PA1 Improve downlink and uplink channel reuse in a given area. PA1 Improve reception of wireless broadcast signals from users by sampling an array of directional antennae to derive the local transmission field strength.
base_text:
Purpose of the invention: General Statement of the problem

This invention aims to enhance the precision with which diverse signals are transmitted to numerous, geographically separated destinations. Specifically, it seeks to improve both downlink and uplink channel reuse within a given area. One of the key innovations lies in refining the reception of wireless broadcast signals from users: by sampling an array of directional antennas, the system can accurately gauge the local transmission field strength.

Current wireless communication systems often struggle with interference and limited channel capacity, largely because of their broad signal coverage and inefficient frequency reuse. The proposed invention overcomes these shortcomings by employing directional antenna arrays that continuously monitor and analyze the local transmission environment. This real-time data enables the system to dynamically adjust the routing and allocation of both uplink and downlink channels.

base_text:
Background
==========

Idiopathic Pulmonary fibrosis (IPF) is a dreadful and incurable, chronic and irreversibly progressive fibrosing lung disease \[[@B1]\]. Inflammation and fibrosis constitute the mainstay of parenchymal lung damage. Despite advances in understanding its pathogenesis, the precise etiology remains elusive. The disease is characterized by excessive deposition of extracellular matrix components, leading to distortion of lung architecture and irreversible loss of function. Common clinical manifestations include progressive dyspnea, chronic dry cough, and eventual respiratory failure. The prognosis of IPF is poor, with a median survival of three to five years following diagnosis, underscoring the urgent need for novel therapeutic strategies and improved disease management \[[@B2]; [@B3]\].
final_text:

base_text:
Q:

Размер плавающего фрейма в ячейке таблицы в зависимости от содержимого

Здравствуйте, коллеги! Мой сайт построен на основе 1 таблицы. Одним из ее элементов является плавающий фрейм. Проблема заключается в том, что размер фрейма (iframe) не подстраивается автоматически под содержимое, и из-за этого появляются полосы прокрутки или пустое пространство. Решить эту задачу можно несколькими способами:

1. **Указать фиксированные размеры iframe**  
   Это самый простой способ, но он не всегда удобен, если содержимое меняется.

   ```html
   <iframe src="page.html" width="500" height="300"></iframe>
   ```

2. **Использовать JavaScript для автоматического изменения размера**  
   Можно добавить скрипт, который будет менять размер iframe в зависимости от содержимого. Однако для этого обе страницы (родительская и вложенная) должны находиться на одном домене из соображений безопасности (Same Origin Policy).

prompt_text: Prevention of alcohol-induced developmental delays and learning abnormalities in a model of fetal alcohol syndrome. Prenatal alcohol exposure results in fetal death and neurobehavioral complications including learning impairment.
base_text: Prevention of alcohol-induced developmental delays and learning abnormalities in a model of fetal alcohol syndrome. Prenatal alcohol exposure results in fetal death and neurobehavioral complications including learning impairment. Research using animal models has demonstrated that exposure to ethanol during critical periods of brain development leads to persistent deficits in cognitive and behavioral functions. These impairments are associated with neuronal loss, altered synaptic plasticity, and disruptions in neurotransmitter systems. Preventive strategies aimed at mitigating the deleterious effects of prenatal alcohol exposure have focused on nutritional supplementation, antioxidant therapy, and pharmacological interventions targeting specific neurodevelopmental pathways. For instance, administration of neuroprotective agents such as choline or antioxidants during gestation has shown promise in reducing alcohol-induced neurotoxicity and improving learning outcomes in offspring. Early identification and intervention remain crucial for minimizing long-term developmental delays and enhancing cognitive function in individuals affected by fetal alcohol syndrome.

The aim of this study was to investigate the effect of N. sativa oil on impaired glucose tolerance and insulin insensitivity induced by high-fat diet and trauma. Three dietary groups were used in this study; Rat-Chow (RC), N. sativa oil diet (Combination 4% N. sativa oil and 16% butter oil) (NSOD) and 20% Butter Oil Diet (BOD). Each group was subdivided in two groups; control and trauma.
base_text: This study set out to examine whether N. sativa oil could counteract the impaired glucose tolerance and insulin resistance brought on by a high-fat diet and trauma. Researchers divided rats into three dietary groups: a standard chow group (RC), a group receiving a diet with 4% N. sativa oil and 16% butter oil (NSOD), and a group fed a diet containing 20% butter oil (BOD). Each dietary group was further split into two: one subgroup served as the control, while the other underwent trauma induced by an intramuscular injection of turpentine oil (0.05 ml/100 g body weight); controls received saline instead.

Each dietary group was further split into two: one subgroup served as the control, while the other underwent trauma injduced by an intramuscular injection of turpentine oil (0.05 ml/100 g body weight); controls received saline instead.
